active: A customer is considered 'active' if they had a successful money movement (transaction) in a given time period. Also called 'Transacting Active'. Use movement_date to aggregate distinct counts of customer_ids in the fact_money_movements table (APP_CASH.APP.FACT_MONEY_MOVEMENTS).
transacting_active: A Cash App account that has at least one financial transaction using any product or service within Cash App during a specified period. Examples include sending/receiving P2P payments, transferring money, making Cash Card purchases, earning stock dividends, paying back loans. Same as 'Active'.
new_active: Customers who sign up for Cash App and make their first successful money movement within a specific time period. For external reporting, 'new actives' is reported instead of just signups.
mau: Monthly Active Users - Customers who have had at least one successful money movement in a given month.
mau+: Customers who are active on a weekly or daily basis. These customers produce distinctly different LTV compared to less frequent users. Approximately 50% of customers use Cash App at least weekly.
established: A customer becomes 'established' when they have 3+ successful money movements in a 30-day window. This is tracked in the ESTABLISHMENT_DATE field.
money_movement: Any successful transaction where money is transferred, including P2P payments, Cash In, Cash Out, Cash Card purchases, Direct Deposits, Bitcoin transactions, Stock transactions, etc. Core metric for defining active customers.
customer_token: Unique text identifier for a Cash App customer, typically prefixed with 'C_'. Primary key for identifying customers across tables.
p2p: Peer-to-Peer payments. The core Cash App feature allowing customers to send and receive money from other Cash App users. Approximately 50% of customers still only use Cash App for P2P. Composed ~44% of inflows as of Dec 2023.
cash_in: When a customer adds money directly into their Cash App wallet by linking an external bank account. Composed ~31% of inflows as of Dec 2023. Often used to fund Bitcoin, Stocks, or other transactions.
cash_out: When a customer transfers money from their Cash App balance to an external bank account or debit card.
cash_card: Cash App's debit card product. Customers can use it for purchases and ATM withdrawals. Cash Card actives are customers who have made at least one Cash Card transaction in the period.
direct_deposit: When a customer's paycheck or other income (tax returns, government assistance, Social Security) is deposited directly into their Cash App account. Composed ~13% of inflows as of Dec 2023. Introduced in Q1 2019.
pda: Paycheck Deposit Actives - Customers who have received $300+ in paychecks or income replacements (Social Security) via direct deposit in a single calendar month. Status maintained for 33 days. Highest product hierarchy in Banking State.
dd: Direct Deposit - Same as direct_deposit. Common abbreviation in queries and table names.
banking_state: Classification of customers based on product adoption and hierarchy: PDA (Paycheck Deposit Actives), Cash Card Actives, P2P+Other Actives, or Inactive. Reflects the extent of relationship a customer has with Cash App.
product_persona: Segmentation based on which Cash App products a customer actively uses. Also called 'Product Attach Persona'. Used for behavioral analysis and forecasting.
inflows: Total amount of money entering Cash App across all channels (P2P, Cash In, Direct Deposit, Paper Money Deposit, Cash Card, C4B, etc.). Key metric in Inflows Framework.
inflows_per_active: Average amount of money each active customer brings into Cash App within a set period. Calculated as total inflows divided by total actives. Core metric for financial planning.
outflows: Total amount of money leaving Cash App (Cash Out, P2P sent, Cash Card purchases, Bitcoin purchases, Stock purchases, etc.).
gpv: Gross Payment Volume - Total dollar value of transactions processed. For P2P, this is the total amount sent. For Cash Card, this is total purchase volume.
c4b: Cash for Business - Business accounts that receive payments via P2P. Separated for management due to different risk loss and churn profiles. Composed ~2.7% of inflows as of Dec 2023.
cap: Cash App Pay - Feature allowing customers to pay at merchant checkouts using their Cash App balance. Also abbreviated as CAP.
pmd: Paper Money Deposit - Customers deposit physical cash at partner locations (CVS, Walgreens, etc.) into their Cash App wallet. Introduced in Q3 2021. Composed ~4% of inflows as of Dec 2023.
oct: Original Credit Transaction - A push payment onto Cash Card that happens when a customer cashes out from another app (Venmo, Uber, DoorDash) directly onto their Cash Card.
instant_deposit: Feature allowing customers to instantly transfer money from Cash App to their linked debit card for a fee. Also called 'ID'.
idv: Identity Verification - Process where customers verify their identity by providing SSN, date of birth, and other personal information. Required for certain features and higher transaction limits. ~25% of customers haven't IDV'd.
birth_year: Customer's year of birth obtained from Identity Verification (IDV). Used to calculate age and generation segments. Only available after successful IDV.
cashtag: Customer's unique Cash App username (e.g., $JohnDoe) that allows others to send them money. Similar to a handle or alias.
blacklisted: Customers who have been blocked/denylisted due to fraud, compliance, or risk issues. Also called 'denylisted'. Check BLACKLISTED or DENYLIST_STATUS fields.
denylisted: Same as blacklisted. Customers blocked from using Cash App due to fraud, compliance, or risk reasons. Filter these out for clean analytics using denylist_status = 'Not Denylisted'.
payment_blocks: Risk action affecting ~35% of established customer cohorts. Affects both authentic and inauthentic customers. Causes the most drag on cohort quality.
teen: Customers under 18 years old with special account restrictions. Part of Families program where a Sponsor (parent/guardian) oversees a Dependent (teen) account.
families: Program allowing parents (Sponsors) to create and manage accounts for their children (Dependents/Teens). Strategic priority for Cash App.
sponsor: Parent or guardian account in the Families program that oversees dependent/teen accounts.
dependent: Teen account in the Families program that is linked to and monitored by a sponsor account.
bitcoin: Cryptocurrency feature allowing customers to buy, sell, and hold Bitcoin. One of Cash App's investing products. Also abbreviated as BTC.
btc: Bitcoin - Cryptocurrency product. Customers can buy, sell, hold, and transfer Bitcoin.
stocks: Investing feature allowing customers to buy and sell stocks and ETFs. Also called 'equities'.
equities: Same as stocks. Investing feature for buying/selling stocks and ETFs.
borrow: Lending feature allowing eligible customers to borrow money from Cash App. Repaid with interest over time.
savings: High-yield savings account feature allowing customers to earn interest on their Cash App balance.
boost: Cash Card rewards program offering instant discounts at select merchants.
network_segment: Geographic segmentation capturing virality and network effects: Self-sustain (strongest), Invest (growing), Reassess (weakest). Critical for Cash App's competitive advantage.
self_sustain: Strongest network segment with highest virality and self-sustaining growth through network effects. Best retention and engagement.
cohort: Group of customers who joined or reached a milestone during the same time period. Used for retention analysis. 2020 and 2021 were largest acquisition cohorts.
movement_cohort: Cohort based on when customers made their first successful money movement. Tracked in MOVEMENT_COHORT_MONTH and MOVEMENT_COHORT_YEAR fields.
establishment_cohort: Cohort based on when customers became 'established' (3+ successful money movements in 30 days).
acquisition_channel: How customers discovered and signed up for Cash App: Organic (largest), Invitations, Referrals, Paid Marketing, Partner Channels.
organic: Largest acquisition channel. Note: Due to iOS privacy changes, 'organic' likely contains some unattributed paid marketing customers.
ltv: Lifetime Value - Predicted total profit a customer will generate over their lifetime with Cash App.
cac: Customer Acquisition Cost - Cost to acquire a new customer through marketing and growth efforts.
churn: When a customer stops using Cash App (becomes inactive). Measured as gross churn rate.
retention: Percentage of customers who remain active over time. Varies by cohort, region, and network segment.
rule_of_40: Block's strategic metric: Growth Rate + Profit Margin should equal or exceed 40%. Key performance indicator.
bank_the_base: Strategic initiative to make Cash App the primary bank for existing customers. Focus on Direct Deposit and Cash Card adoption.
primary_banking_actives: Customers using Cash App as their primary bank. Highest value segment. Also called PBA.
pba: Primary Banking Actives - Customers using Cash App as their main financial account.
income_segment: Segmentation based on zip code household income: 0-30k, 30k-70k, 70k+. Designed to align with banking product strategy.
core_customers: Combination of Access + Simplicity + Growth customer insight segments, OR customers with income 30k-70k and/or Self-sustain network. Represents 50% of customer base.
habituation: Measure of how consistently customers use Cash App. Segments: Daily Actives (10%), Weekly Actives (~50%), Monthly Actives, Occasional Users.
blocker: Any screen or popup that appears in the Cash App requiring customer action. Tracked via BlockerFlow Interact events.
flow_token: Identifier used to stitch together customer actions and screens in the app. Found in client event data.
client_events: Events tracked on the customer's device (mobile app). Viewable in Amplitude. Examples: Asset Send Start, Asset Send Submit Transaction.
server_events: Events tracked on Cash App's servers. Complement client events for complete transaction tracking.
cdf: Customer Data Framework - Generalized structure for all event reporting (client and server) across the Cash App.
amplitude: Analytics tool for viewing customer events and user behavior. Access at go/amplitude with Cash CDP project.
movement_date: Date when a money movement (transaction) occurred. Use this field to aggregate active customers.
fact_money_movements: Core table containing all successful money activity movements for Cash App. Located at APP_CASH.APP.FACT_MONEY_MOVEMENTS.
customer_activity_base: Key table containing monthly Inflows Framework metrics at customer level with important segmentations. Located at APP_CASH.INFLOWS_FRAMEWORK.CUSTOMER_ACTIVITY_BASE.
dim_customer_segmentations: Centralized table for customer demographics, behavioral, and geographic attributes. Located at APP_CASH.INFLOWS_FRAMEWORK.DIM_CUSTOMER_SEGMENTATIONS.
customer_profile: Comprehensive customer profile table with identification, account status, demographics, device usage, payment methods, and risk indicators. Located at APP_CASH.GLOBAL.CUSTOMER_PROFILE.
metrics_store: Centralized repository for Cash App metrics with governance and semantic layer. Block Metrics Store (BMS) is the single source of truth.
cms: Cash Metrics Store - Legacy metrics system being migrated to Block Metrics Store (BMS).
bms: Block Metrics Store - New unified metrics platform with semantic layer abstraction. Single source of truth for metrics across Block.
mma: Money Movement Activity - Rollup tables containing aggregated transaction data. Foundation for metrics calculations.
tier_1_metrics: Most important Cash App metrics including total_actives, total_inflows_usd, cash_app_card_actives, paycheck_deposit_actives, p2p_gpv_sent.
mtd: Month-to-Date - Metrics calculated from the beginning of the current month to the current date.
yoy: Year-over-Year - Comparison of metrics to the same period in the previous year.
qoq: Quarter-over-Quarter - Comparison of metrics between consecutive quarters.
attach_rate: Percentage of customers who adopt a specific product. Example: Banking attach rate = customers with banking products / total actives.
cross_border: Transactions involving different countries. Tracked for compliance and monitoring purposes.
velocity_metrics: Transaction monitoring metrics tracking transaction frequency, amounts, and patterns over lookback periods. Used for fraud and compliance.
lookback_period: Time window for analyzing transaction patterns, typically 30, 60, or 91 days.
typology: Classification of transaction pattern or risk type. Used in compliance and fraud detection.
counterparty: The other party in a transaction. Number of unique counterparties tracked for risk assessment.
gross_profit: Revenue minus direct costs. Key profitability metric tracked by product line (P2P, Cash Card, Bitcoin, etc.).
net_revenue: Total revenue after transaction costs and refunds. Used in financial reporting.
monetization_rate: Revenue generated per dollar of inflows or GPV. Key metric in Inflows Framework.
inflows_framework: Cash App's business operating model showing interplay between Actives, Inflows per Active, and Monetization Rate.
banking_suite: Collection of banking products including Cash Card, Direct Deposit, Savings. Leads multi-product growth.
multi_product: Customers using 2+ Cash App products. Higher engagement and retention than single-product users.
product_adoption: When a customer starts using a new Cash App feature or product. Tracked for growth analysis.
penetration: Percentage of target market or geography using Cash App. High penetration areas have stronger network effects.
msa: Metropolitan Statistical Area - Geographic designation used for regional analysis and network segmentation.
dma: Designated Market Area - Geographic region used for advertising and media buying. Tracked in LOC_DMA field.
wallet_share: Portion of a customer's financial activity conducted through Cash App vs other financial services.
share_of_wallet: Same as wallet_share. How much of customer's financial life is on Cash App.
kyc: Know Your Customer - Compliance process for verifying customer identity. Part of IDV process.
kyb: Know Your Business - Compliance process for verifying business customer identity. Required for C4B accounts.
aml: Anti-Money Laundering - Compliance monitoring to detect and prevent money laundering activities.
tm: Transaction Monitoring - Compliance system tracking transaction patterns for suspicious activity.
rate_plan: Fee structure and pricing plan assigned to a customer account. Affects transaction costs.
instrument: Payment method linked to Cash App account (bank account, debit card, credit card). Also called 'funding source'.
funding_source: Payment method used to fund transactions. Can be bank account, debit card, credit card, or Cash App balance.
stored_balance: Money held in customer's Cash App wallet. Can be used to fund transactions without external payment method.
ach: Automated Clearing House - Bank transfer method. Used for Direct Deposit and bank account transfers.
wire_transfer: Electronic funds transfer method. Tracked separately from ACH. Composed small portion of inflows.
check_deposit: Feature allowing customers to deposit checks by taking photos. Part of 'Other' inflows category.
gift_cards: Feature allowing customers to buy and redeem gift cards. Part of 'Other' inflows category.
refund: Money returned to customer from merchant or reversed transaction. Tracked as inflow when it hits Cash Card.
chargeback: Disputed transaction reversed by card network. Tracked for risk and compliance.
dispute: Customer-initiated challenge to a transaction. Includes misdirected payments and unauthorized charges.
fraud: Unauthorized or deceptive transactions. Major focus area for risk and compliance teams.
scam: Fraudulent scheme targeting customers. Perceptions of scams on Cash App outweigh actual scam rates.
trust: Customer confidence in Cash App's security and reliability. Critical for adoption and retention. Four facets tracked.
balance_confusion: Customer pain point around understanding their Cash App balance across different wallets (Cash, Bitcoin, Stocks).
personalization: Feature allowing customers to add messages, emojis, or customization to P2P payments.
boost_activation: When a customer activates a Boost reward on their Cash Card.
paylink: Shareable payment link allowing customers to request money from multiple people.
pools: Feature allowing groups to collect money together for shared expenses or goals.
tax: Cash App Tax - Free tax filing service for customers. Seasonal product with spikes in Q1.
neighborhoods: Feature connecting customers with local businesses and community. Part of commerce strategy.
exposium: Legacy data pipeline system being replaced by Block Metrics Store. Used for metric calculations.
prefect: Workflow orchestration tool used for data pipelines and ETL processes.
etl: Extract, Transform, Load - Data pipeline process for moving and transforming data.
squarewave: Block's internal data pipeline and job orchestration platform.
mode: Business intelligence and SQL analytics tool. Used for dashboards and ad-hoc analysis. Access at modeanalytics.com/cashapp.
looker: Business intelligence platform for dashboards and reporting. Used across Block.
app_cash: Primary Snowflake database for Cash App data. Contains schemas like APP, INFLOWS_FRAMEWORK, METRICS_STORE, GLOBAL.
app_compliance: Snowflake database for compliance and risk data. Contains denylists, transaction monitoring, and regulatory data.
quant_finance: Snowflake database for quantitative finance team data, including forecasting models and financial analysis.
robot_user: Service account used for automated data pipelines and ETL jobs. Used for cost allocation and access control.
payment_token: Unique identifier for a Cash App Pay payment, typically prefixed with 'PWC_'. Primary key for CAP payment analysis. A single payment_token can have multiple rows with incremental auth.
asr: Authorization Success Rate - Number of authorized payments divided by total payment attempts. Key CAP metric. Formula: (Authorized Payments / Total Attempted Payments). High ASR indicates healthy payment ecosystem.
deduped_asr: De-duplicated Authorization Success Rate - ASR calculated after removing duplicate declined payment attempts. More accurate measure than raw ASR. Removes duplicate declines based on customer, merchant, amount, and time window.
rsr: Registration Success Rate - Rate at which customers successfully generate a grant after indicating intention to pay. Also called Grant Approval Rate. Formula: (Processed Grants / Created Grants).
tsr: Transaction Success Rate - Same as ASR in Cash App terminology. Measures merchant's success at attempting a payment.
payment_state: Current state of a CAP payment: AUTHORIZED, DECLINED, VOIDED, CAPTURED, REFUNDED. Use for filtering payments by status.
transaction_state: State of individual transaction attempts, especially for incremental auth. Can be AUTHORIZED or DECLINED even when payment_state is AUTHORIZED.
sequence_num: Sequence number for incremental authorization attempts. sequence_num = 0 is initial auth, sequence_num > 0 indicates incremental auth attempts.
incremental_auth: Incremental Authorization - Allows merchants to increase authorization amount for pending CAP payment. Requires initial auth to be successful and merchant to have feature enabled.
authorized: Payment state when MerBro has obtained authorization from payment processor, risk approved, and customer won't exceed limits. Money is reserved and guaranteed.
declined: Payment state when authorization or pre-requisites failed. Common reasons: risk block, exceeding limits, insufficient funds. Terminal state.
voided: Payment state when merchant cancels payment before capturing. Payment must be in authorized or declined state. Terminal state (except eager voids count as authorized in ASR).
captured: Payment state when MerBro has captured (settled) money with payment processor. Minimum capture amount is $0.01. Can have partial refunds.
refunded: Payment state when full refund linked to payment has been successfully authorized. Terminal state. Can also have partial refunds where refunded_amount < captured_amount.
eager_void: Payment automatically voided by MerBro to clean up failed authorization or when SLA timeout occurs (>2.65 seconds). Failure_reason = 'INTERNAL_EAGER_VOID'. Considered authorized in ASR formula.
manually_voided: Payment manually voided by merchant or system. Failure_reason = 'MANUALLY_VOIDED'.
expired_authorization_auto_void: MerBro automatically voided payment with expired authorization. Failure_reason = 'EXPIRED_AUTHORIZATION_AUTO_VOID'.
failure_reason: Primary reason for payment decline. Examples: AUTHORIZATION_FAILURE, RISK_BLOCKED, LIMITS_FAILURE, INSTRUMENT_FAILURE. Multiple reasons can apply to single decline.
external_failure_reason: Detailed failure reason from payment processor for AUTHORIZATION_FAILURE declines. Hundreds of unique values, cleaned into external_failure_reason_clean for analysis.
external_failure_reason_clean: Simplified, human-readable categories for external_failure_reason. Rare reasons (<1% of declines) grouped into 'Other'.
authorization_failure: Payment authorization failed at processor level. Most common decline reason. Check external_failure_reason for details.
risk_blocked: Payment blocked by Risk team due to fraud or compliance concerns. Failure_reason = 'RISK_BLOCKED'.
limits_failure: Customer exceeded spending limits (daily, weekly, monthly, or single transaction). Failure_reason = 'LIMITS_FAILURE'.
instrument_failure: Failed to retrieve valid funding instrument for payment. Rare, typically results in AUTHORIZATION_FAILURE.
nsf: Non-Sufficient Funds - Customer doesn't have enough money in Cash App balance or linked account to cover transaction. Common decline reason.
prohibited_purchase_mcc_blocked: Customer not allowed to make purchase because CAP doesn't support this merchant category code (MCC). Failure_reason = 'PROHIBITED_PURCHASE_MCC_BLOCKED'.
prohibited_purchase_underage: Customer not allowed to make purchase because they are underage (teen accounts). Failure_reason = 'PROHIBITED_PURCHASE_UNDERAGE'.
client_error: Payment failed due to bad request from external client (PSP). Failure_reason = 'CLIENT_ERROR'.
sla_timeout: Payment took too long to process (>2.65 seconds), triggering eager void to meet SLA requirements. Part of partnership agreements requiring <1% of payments over 3 seconds.
duplicate_decline: Declined payment flagged as duplicate based on same customer, merchant, amount within specified time window (15 min to 30 days). Used for calculating de-duped ASR.
unique_decline: Declined payment not flagged as duplicate. First decline in a series or doesn't match duplicate criteria.
overcapture: Captured amount greater than authorized amount. Default allows up to 20% overcapture, adjustable by merchant (e.g., Domino's has 400% limit).
undercapture: Captured amount less than authorized amount. No lower threshold (e.g., auth $20, capture $1 is valid).
authorized_amount: Dollar amount authorized for payment. Found in authorized_amount:amount field.
captured_amount: Dollar amount actually captured/settled for payment. Found in captured_amount:amount field. Can differ from authorized_amount.
refunded_amount: Dollar amount refunded to customer. Can be partial (< captured_amount) or full (= captured_amount).
amount_cents: Payment amount in cents. Used for duplicate detection and analysis.
discount_amount_cents: Discount applied to payment, also in MARKETING movement_type. Cash marketing account pays discount portion.
svb: Stored Value Balance - Amount of money in customer's Cash App account. Can be used for CAP payments if customer is IDV'd and has sufficient funds.
stored_value: Post-discount payment amount pulled from customer's SVB. Movement_type = 'STORED_VALUE' in money movements table.
card: Post-discount payment amount pulled from customer's linked debit card when unable to use SVB. Movement_type = 'CARD'.
fee: Fee charged for using CAP as a service (revenue). Movement_type = 'FEE'.
marketing: Discount applied to payment. Movement_type = 'MARKETING'. Cash marketing account pays this portion.
payment_push: Amount merchant receives for payment (total amount minus fee). CAP pays merchant through PSP/client. Movement_type = 'PAYMENT PUSH'.
risk_fund: When customer disputes payment and merchant challenges with evidence, CAP treats as both parties winning. Merchant doesn't pay, CAP pays customer from risk fund. Movement_type = 'RISK FUND'.
parent_merchant_token: Identifier for parent merchant in CAP transactions. Used for merchant-level analysis and duplicate detection.
merchant_broker: MerBro - Merchant Broker system handling CAP payment processing and authorization. See go/merbro for details.
merbro: Merchant Broker - System managing CAP payment lifecycle from authorization through capture. Abbreviated as MerBro.
psp: Payment Service Provider - External service processing CAP payments for merchants. Examples: Stripe, Adyen.
plasma: Cash App's payment processing system. See Plasma Overview for architecture details.
grant: Permission granted by customer to merchant to attempt payment. Part of CAP authorization flow.
grant_approval_rate: Rate at which customers approve top-up and allow merchants to attempt payment. Formula: (Processed Grants / Created Grants). Same as RSR.
ittu: In-Transaction Top-Up - Process of adding funds to Cash App account during a transaction to cover payment amount.
decline_prevention: Detecting transactions before they decline due to insufficient funds and notifying customer to add fixed amount to SVB. Also called DP.
decline_recovery: Notification system routing customers to add cash keypad with delta between last NSF transaction amount and current balance prefilled.
aft: Account Funding Transaction - Act of adding funds to Cash App account. Also called Cash-Ins. Methods include bank link, receiving payments, direct deposit, adding cash at retail.
cit: Customer Initiated Transaction - Transaction initiated by customer from available payment sources to purchase product/service. Example: Customer ordering food on DoorDash.
mit: Merchant Initiated Transaction - Transaction initiated by merchant from authorized payment source to recover scheduled payment (usually subscription). Example: DoorDash charging $9.99/month for DashPass.
aov: Average Order Value - Average value of every CAP transaction. Formula: Total GPV / Number of transactions. Example: $100 GPV over 10 transactions = $10 AOV.
agpv: Addressable GPV - GPV associated with portion of Total Addressable Market (TAM) that will be impacted by feature/change.
gross_margin: For decline prevention: Revenue - (Top-up processing fees + Marketing fees). For non-DP: Revenue - (Card network processing fees + Marketing fees).
interchange_fee: Fees paid by merchant's bank (acquirer) to cardholder's bank (issuer) for processing transactions.
mcc: Merchant Category Code - Standard code classifying merchant business type. Some MCCs declined for all users (7995, 4815, 5432) or teens (7800-7802, 5921, 5813, 3000-3999).
client_id: Identifier for specific CAP use case or integration. Example: 'CA-CI_AFTERPAY-REPAYMENT' for Afterpay repayment transactions.
cash_app_pay_transactions: Master table for all CAP transactions across Merbro and Franklin. Located at APP_CASH.APP.CASH_APP_PAY_TRANSACTIONS. Single source of truth.
cap_deduplicate_declined_payment: Table with logic to flag declines as unique/duplicate based on customer, amount, merchant, time windows. Located at APP_CASH.APP.CAP_DEDUPLICATE_DECLINED_PAYMENT. Used for calculating de-duped ASR.
cash_app_pay_incr_auth_transactions: Payment_token and sequence_num level table for CAP incremental auth attempts. Located at CASH_COMMERCE.PROD.CASH_APP_PAY_INCR_AUTH_TRANSACTIONS.
commerce_transactions_non_pii: Payment_token level table for CAP from payment events. Located at CASH_COMMERCE.PROD_RAW.COMMERCE_TRANSACTIONS_NON_PII. Includes all payment states, source for cash_app_pay_transactions.
paywithcash_money_movements: Payment_token level table for successful CAP payments. Located at APP_CASH.CASH_DATA_BOT.PAYWITHCASH_MONEY_MOVEMENTS. Source for cash_app_pay_transactions.
occurred_at: Timestamp when payment event occurred. Use for time-based filtering and analysis of CAP payments.
load_timestamp: Timestamp when record was loaded into table. Use for incremental data processing and freshness checks.
dupe_24_hour: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 24-hour window. Used in de-duped ASR calculations.
dupe_15_min: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 15-minute window.
dupe_1_hour: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 1-hour window.
dupe_7_days: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 7-day window.
dupe_14_days: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 14-day window.
dupe_28_days: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 28-day window.
dupe_30_days: Boolean flag indicating if decline is duplicate within 30-day window.
cap_kpi_splits: Primary CAP metrics dashboard showing GPV, transactions, customers, RSR, ASR by various splits (PSP, account type, age). First stop for CAP metrics. See go/cap_data.
cap_payment_declines_deep_dive: Dashboard with in-depth view of CAP payment declines (raw and de-duped) by customer demographics (teen vs adult, instrument type, channel, platform).
asr_improvement_analysis: Dashboard analyzing CAP ASR, declines, and re-attempted successful payments after initial decline. Used to identify ASR improvement opportunities.
cap_incremental_auth_dashboard: Merchant-specific dashboard tracking incremental auth metrics (ASR, incremental GPV, usage rate). Example: Lyft.
dispute_tracker: Dashboard for in-depth analysis of CAP dispute counts, amounts, and trends for merchants.
paydash: Real-time monitoring dashboard for CAP payments. See go/$paydash.
